teachingPoint: Distributed billing cycles require a status check before acquiring a lock. Acquiring the lock before checking subscription status wastes the lock slot on cancelled subscriptions and delays detection — the cancelled-account charge happens after a pointless lock acquisition. Status check must be ORDER 1; lock acquisition is ORDER 2.
positiveExample: T211 calls IBillingScheduleService.getStatus() at order 1; on CANCELLED → emits InvoiceVoided immediately, no lock acquired. Lock acquired at order 2 only for ACTIVE/PAST_DUE subscriptions.
negativeExample: T211 acquires lock first, then checks status. CANCELLED subscription consumes lock slot, and the status check that fires after can still proceed to charge in a race condition if another event arrives between lock and check.
discriminatingConstraint: status check at ORDER 1, lock at ORDER 2. Lock before status = silent charge on cancelled subscription.
keywords: billing lock status check order cancelled subscription double charge distributed lock SETNX

teachingPoint: Monetary values must be stored as integer cents, never as floating-point. IEEE 754 double-precision arithmetic produces rounding errors on multiplication and division (9.99 × 12 = 119.88000000000001). Over months of proration calculations, drift accumulates into invoice amounts that don't match the contract price. The MACHINE rule is: store priceCents as integer, apply Math.floor to all division operations.
positiveExample: T209 iron rule IR-1: Number.isInteger(event.priceCents) && event.priceCents > 0. Float prices (9.99) rejected at ORDER 1 before any write. T211 proration uses Math.floor for all partial-period calculations.
negativeExample: T209 accepts price: 9.99 as a float. After 6 months of monthly proration cycles the accumulated invoice amounts diverge from the stated plan price by cents, failing financial audits.
discriminatingConstraint: priceCents must be Number.isInteger() — float prices accepted = score-0.
keywords: integer cents price float arithmetic rounding drift proration financial precision IEEE 754

teachingPoint: Dunning retry intervals must come from FREEDOM config, not from code. Hardcoded retry delays require a code deployment to change — which takes hours during a payment processor incident. FREEDOM config key 'subscription_billing_dunning_schedule' allows operators to adjust retry timing in response to processor downtime without touching code.
positiveExample: T211 reads dunningSchedule = await this.freedomConfig.get('subscription_billing_dunning_schedule'). Adjustment during an incident: update config record → effective on next retry cycle, no deploy.
negativeExample: T211 hardcodes const DUNNING_DELAYS = [24, 72, 168] // hours. Payment processor has outage for 30 hours. Day-1 retry fails (processor down). Config cannot be changed without deploy. All day-3 retries also fail before deploy completes.
discriminatingConstraint: dunning schedule from FREEDOM config key — never a const or inline array.
keywords: dunning retry schedule FREEDOM config hardcoded incident response payment processor billing

teachingPoint: Subscription state machine transitions must be idempotent. If SubscribeRequested arrives twice for the same (subscriberId, planId) pair, only one subscription must be created. SETNX on subscriptionId = hash(tenantId+subscriberId+planId) prevents the second write. The rejected duplicate returns SubscriptionAlreadyActive without modifying state.
positiveExample: T210 SETNX key = hash(tenantId+subscriberId+planId) at ORDER 3. Second SubscribeRequested returns DataProcessResult.failure('ALREADY_SUBSCRIBED') without any state write.
negativeExample: T210 skips idempotency check. Network retry causes SubscribeRequested to arrive twice. Two subscription records created for same member. Both bill independently → double charge on renewal.
discriminatingConstraint: SETNX idempotency before state write — idempotency missing = duplicate subscription creation on retry.
keywords: idempotency SETNX state machine transition duplicate subscription retry network

teachingPoint: MRR aggregation must normalize all billing intervals to a monthly equivalent before summing. An annual-plan subscriber paying $1,200/year contributes $100/month to MRR — not $1,200/month. The normalization formula is MACHINE (not configurable): monthly=price, annual=Math.floor(price/12), custom=Math.floor(price/intervalDays*30). Reporting un-normalized MRR inflates the metric by up to 12× for annual-heavy cohorts.
positiveExample: T212 applies normalizeMrr(priceCents, billingInterval, intervalDays) before any aggregation. Annual subscriber paying 120000 cents contributes 10000 cents/month to MRR.
negativeExample: T212 sums priceCents directly without normalization. Annual subscriber paying $1,200 counted as $1,200 MRR contribution per month. 10 annual subscribers inflate MRR from $2,000 to $14,000.
discriminatingConstraint: MRR = Math.floor(priceCents/12) for annual plans — raw priceCents contribution = inflated MRR, score-0.
keywords: MRR normalization annual plan monthly equivalent billing interval proration revenue metric inflated

teachingPoint: A subscription billing aggregator must subscribe to both SubscriptionActivated (add MRR) and SubscriptionCancelled (subtract MRR). Subscribing to activations only produces permanently inflating MRR — churned subscribers never leave the metric. This is the first billing-domain additive-subtractive pattern in the corpus; FLOW-10 T171 established the pattern for review scores.
positiveExample: T212 has two @EventPattern handlers: SubscriptionActivated (calls recalculate with ADD) and SubscriptionCancelled (calls recalculate with SUBTRACT). Churned subscribers' normalized MRR contribution is removed.
negativeExample: T212 only subscribes to SubscriptionActivated. After 6 months of mixed activations and cancellations, MRR is permanently higher than actual revenue — churn never reflected.
discriminatingConstraint: SubscriptionCancelled handler required — additive-only MRR aggregation score-0.
keywords: MRR churn additive subtractive cancellation aggregation FLOW-10 pattern billing domain
billing lock before charge status check cancelled subscription double charge distributed SETNX scheduled job
teachingPoint: In scheduled billing, status check must precede lock acquisition, and lock must precede all financial operations. This inverts FLOW-09 seat-before-payment — no pre-existing record exists to create first. Both guards are required together: status prevents cancelled-account charges; lock prevents concurrent execution double-charges.
ironRules: Status check is ORDER 1 — CANCELLED/PAUSED → InvoiceVoided immediately, no lock
Lock acquisition is ORDER 2 — lock=false (already running) → return without charge
Invoice generation is ORDER 3 — only after both status and lock confirm proceed
Charge is ORDER 4 — after invoice ID established as idempotency key

keywords: integer cents price float prevention monetary value IEEE 754 rounding proration drift financial
teachingPoint: Monetary values in financial systems must be stored as integer cents (999 not 9.99). Float arithmetic produces IEEE 754 rounding drift that accumulates over proration and renewal cycles. The validation guard is Number.isInteger(priceCents) at ORDER 1 — before SETNX or any write. All division in proration uses Math.floor.
ironRules: Number.isInteger(priceCents) && priceCents > 0 — checked at ORDER 1 before SETNX
Float prices (9.99) rejected at gateway — never stored in ES
All proration division uses Math.floor: Math.floor(priceCents / 12)
priceCents field type is 'integer' in every event schema — not 'number'

keywords: dunning retry schedule FREEDOM config billing incident response payment processor downtime wait hours
teachingPoint: Dunning retry intervals must come from FREEDOM config, not code. A payment processor incident may require extending or collapsing retry windows without a deployment. The FREEDOM config key 'subscription_billing_dunning_schedule' stores [{attempt, wait_hours}] tuples. Key name is MACHINE; values are FREEDOM. Max attempt count also comes from the same array length.
ironRules: maxAttempts = dunningSchedule.length — never a hardcoded constant
Key name 'subscription_billing_dunning_schedule' is MACHINE — never read from a variable

keywords: MRR monthly normalization annual plan billing interval proration revenue aggregation Math.floor
teachingPoint: MRR aggregation must normalize all billing intervals to a monthly equivalent before summing. The formula is MACHINE: monthly=price, annual=Math.floor(price/12), custom=Math.floor(price/intervalDays*30). This formula is NOT configurable — it is a financial definition, not a business rule.
ironRules: Formula is MACHINE — not read from FREEDOM config
Same normalizeMrr() function called on both ADD (activation) and SUBTRACT (cancellation) paths
